question: Is there exactly one standard six-sided die clearly depicted as the main subject?
Is the surface beneath the die identifiable as a wooden table with visible wood grain?
Are the markings on the die faces standard pips (dots) representing numbers between 1 and 6?
Are the pip patterns on each visible face arranged in the standard configuration (e.g., 1 centered pip, diagonal arrangement for 2, etc.)?
Are exactly three faces of the die visible in the image?
Do the three visible faces share a common corner vertex (consistent with the geometry of a cube)?
Do the visible faces adhere to the standard die rule where opposite faces sum to 7 (i.e., pairs 1&6, 2&5, and 3&4 are never adjacent)?
Is the die resting flat on the table surface with proper contact (not floating)?
Is the geometric shape of the die a proper cube with straight edges and no significant warping?
